evidence: The build stays single-focus per frame: the meter arc alone in frame 1, then columns appear, and by frames 5-6 the '58' readout is the bright center while the you/the-role columns recede to dim support.
Elements accumulate gradually across frames (meter, then two-row columns, then a third row, then products, then readout), so no more than a few novel items land at equal weight in any one frame.
Each value sits on its own labeled row ('Python hours: 12', 'SQL reps: 5', 'nights free: 2') under the 'you' and 'the role' headers, with no separate legend or key.
'two arrows in, one number out' annotates the '58' readout directly below it and 'Different aisle, same meter.' captions the shared meter it refers to.
All row labels, product numbers, the '58' readout, and both caption lines are rendered at comfortably readable size.
Both columns, the meter, and all captions sit fully inside the frame with generous margins.
No text, product numbers, or labels overlap; the columns, center products, and meter occupy clearly separated regions.
Observed row values are consistently periwinkle blue while the pooled products, meter needle, and '58' readout are consistently lilac, matching the parameter/readout role throughout.
The half-dial meter is a load-bearing scoring device, not decoration, and every other element is a label or value.

The promised columns swap to 'you' and 'the role' with Python hours / SQL reps / nights free at readable size, matched entries multiply into a shared lilac readout, and both 'two arrows in, one number out' and 'Different aisle, same meter' land.
Products sit centered between the two columns and the '58' readout sits directly under the meter, keeping each claim beside its object.
Across the six frames the diagram builds in reasoning order: idle meter, columns, added third row, products, then the summed readout and closing line.
The layout keeps wide side and top margins with clear separation between the two columns, the central products, and the meter below.
